studios.location: Leamington Spa, UK (offices in Bangalore, Beijing)

key_decision_maker.name: David Darling
key_decision_maker.title: Founder & CEO
key_decision_maker.background: Co-founder of Codemasters (sold for ~$1B), games industry pioneer, CBE

recent_news.headline: Kwalee receives minority equity funding from Devolver Digital

studios.funding_status: Minority investment from Devolver Digital (July 2024), previously bootstrapped
studios.why_fit: Hyper-casual specialist with proven track record, strong leadership from Codemasters legacy, actively publishing external developer games, recent investment signals growth appetite

key_decision_maker.name: Gilberto Marcal
key_decision_maker.title: CEO
key_decision_maker.background: Leading Lion Studios under Miniclip ownership

recent_news.headline: Lion Studios continues hyper-casual publishing under Miniclip umbrella
recent_news.notes: Strong focus on puzzle and hyper-casual games with global reach

studios.why_fit: 2B+ downloads demonstrates massive scale, focused on hyper-casual partnerships with external developers, offices in 3 major gaming hubs, actively seeking new developer partnerships

studios.location: Los Angeles (HQ), Barcelona, Seoul, Tokyo, Dublin

key_decision_maker.name: Bobby Loya
key_decision_maker.title: SVP, Head of Studios Business Development & Developer Relations
key_decision_maker.background: 20+ year industry veteran, former Activision Blizzard, joined March 2024
key_decision_maker.notes: Primary contact for developer partnerships and M&A

secondary_contact.name: Shlomi Aizenberg
secondary_contact.title: Chief Business Officer
secondary_contact.notes: Oversees post-deal integration and operational growth

recent_news.headline: Scopely completes acquisition of Niantic's games business including Pokemon GO for $3.5B
Scopely appoints Bobby Loya as SVP Head of Studios Business Development

studios.funding_status: Acquired by Savvy Games Group for $4.9B (July 2023)
studios.why_fit: Aggressive M&A strategy with deep Saudi funding, now #1 mobile games company in US, actively building external dev partnerships via new BD leadership, just acquired Pokemon GO - signaling massive live ops appetite

key_decision_maker.name: Burak Vardal
key_decision_maker.title: Co-Founder & CEO
key_decision_maker.background: Former EMEA Growth lead at AdColony, led Rollic to 8 #1/#2 US App Store hits

recent_news.headline: Zynga completes acquisition of Rollic for $168M initial + earnout
recent_news.notes: Zynga's first hyper-casual entry, brought 250M+ monthly active users

studios.why_fit: Rollic is Zynga's dedicated hyper-casual arm, one of fastest-growing publishers pre-acquisition, strong network of external developers, represents Zynga's gateway into hyper-casual partnerships

key_decision_maker.name: Yegor Vaikhanski
key_decision_maker.title: CEO & Co-Founder
key_decision_maker.background: Co-founder of IT English, built SayGames from 0 to 7B+ downloads

recent_news.headline: SayGames profiled by Invest Cyprus - 150+ games and 7B downloads
recent_news.notes: Major employer in Cyprus with hubs in Limassol, Paphos, Warsaw

studios.why_fit: One of the largest hyper-casual publishers globally (7B+ downloads), actively expanding hybrid-casual portfolio, recent high-profile media coverage signals growth phase, massive scale with relatively lean team (200+)

priority_targets: Kwalee (recent funding, strong leadership, UK presence)
